questions.question: Which among these is a post-harvest technology suitable for women farmers?
questions.subject: Agricultural Engineering / Post-harvest Technology
questions.explanation: A grain cleaner-cum-grader performs cleaning and size grading of grain with comparatively low drudgery and is suitable for small-scale post-harvest operations, including use by women farmers.

questions.question: How can climate change lead to irrigated agriculture?

options.B: By increasing crop resistance to pests
options.C: By causing extreme weather patterns and water scarcity

questions.subject: Agrometeorology / Climate Change
questions.explanation: Climate change can make rainfall more erratic and increase the frequency of drought and heat. These conditions increase water scarcity and dependence on irrigation for reliable crop production.

questions.question: Groundnut belongs to which group based on economic use
questions.subject: Agronomy / Crop Classification
questions.explanation: Groundnut is classified as an edible oilseed crop because its kernels contain a high proportion of oil and are cultivated mainly for oil extraction and food use.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 36.

questions.question: Which of the following is a micronutrient for plants?
questions.subject: Soil Fertility / Plant Nutrition
questions.explanation: Manganese is required by plants in small quantities and is classified as an essential micronutrient. Phosphorus, potassium and calcium are macronutrients.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 193.

questions.question: What is the major factor affecting water use efficiency in plants?
questions.subject: Agronomy / Water-use Efficiency
questions.topic: Canopy influence on water use efficiency
questions.explanation: Leaf area index determines the size of the photosynthetic and transpiring canopy. An optimum leaf area improves interception of radiation and biomass production relative to water used.

questions.question: Which form of the soil water is available to plants?
questions.subject: Soil Physics / Soil Water
questions.explanation: Capillary water is retained in soil micropores against gravity as a continuous film around soil particles and is available for plant growth. Gravitational water drains freely, while hygroscopic water is held too tightly for plant use.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 123.

questions.question: What is the average duration (maturity) of pearl millet crop
questions.subject: Agronomy / Pearl Millet
questions.explanation: Pearl millet is a short-duration warm-season cereal. Early cultivars commonly mature in about 60-75 days, which makes the crop suitable for dryland and contingency planting.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of napier grass?

options.D: Can be harvested multiple times a year

questions.subject: Agronomy / Fodder Crops
questions.explanation: Napier grass is a high-yielding perennial fodder grass that can be cut repeatedly and tolerates moisture stress after establishment. Being a non-legume, it does not naturally enrich soil nitrogen through symbiotic fixation.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 36.

questions.question: One of the most serious insect/pests affecting the yield of cowpea is
questions.subject: Entomology / Pulse Pests
questions.explanation: Pod borers damage cowpea by feeding on flowers, developing pods and seeds, directly reducing marketable grain yield.

questions.question: Sunflower oil is considered healthy due to its high content of
questions.subject: Agronomy / Oilseed Quality
questions.explanation: Sunflower oil is valued for its high proportion of polyunsaturated fatty acids. The book specifically notes that PUFA content is highest in sunflower.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 16.

questions.question: The species of rhizobium associated with the cultivation of chickpea crop is
questions.subject: Agronomy / Soil Microbiology
questions.explanation: Chickpea forms effective root nodules with its specific Rhizobium group. Rhizobium ciceri establishes symbiosis with chickpea roots and fixes atmospheric nitrogen for use by the crop.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method of curing tobacco?
questions.subject: Agronomy / Tobacco
questions.explanation: Curing is an essential post-harvest operation in tobacco. Depending on the tobacco type and the desired leaf quality, curing is carried out by sun curing, fire curing, flue curing or air curing.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 7.

questions.question: Which part of the Parthenium weed plant contains the highest concentration of allelochemicals?
questions.subject: Weed Science / Allelopathy
questions.explanation: Parthenium releases allelochemicals from its plant parts, and the leaves contain a high concentration of these compounds. Leaf residues and leachates therefore exert strong inhibitory effects on neighbouring plants.
questions.reference: Fundamentals of Agriculture, Vol. 1 - Arun Katyayan, Book page 24.

questions.question: What type of adjuvant helps herbicides resist being washed off by rain shortly after application
questions.subject: Weed Science / Pesticide Application
questions.explanation: Stickers increase the adhesion of spray droplets to the plant surface. They reduce washing of the applied herbicide by rainfall and improve retention of the spray deposit on foliage.

questions.question: The main sweetening compounds in Stevia leaves are
questions.subject: Medicinal and Aromatic Crops
questions.explanation: The sweetness of Stevia leaves is due mainly to steviol glycosides, especially stevioside and rebaudioside compounds. These glycosides are intensely sweet and are used as non-caloric sweeteners.

questions.question: Which is the major pest affecting coconut plant?
questions.subject: Entomology / Plantation Crop Pests
questions.explanation: The rhinoceros beetle is an important pest of coconut. Adults bore into the crown and unopened fronds, producing characteristic cuts in the emerging leaves and weakening the palm.

questions.question: Aonla is highly tolerant to
questions.subject: Horticulture / Fruit Crops
questions.explanation: Aonla is a hardy fruit crop adapted to dry and semi-arid conditions. Its deep root system and hardy growth enable it to withstand prolonged drought better than waterlogging.

questions.question: An LER value greater than 1 in the intercropping system indicates
questions.subject: Agronomy / Cropping Systems
questions.explanation: Land equivalent ratio compares the area required under sole crops with the area used in intercropping to produce the same yield. An LER greater than one indicates a yield advantage and more efficient land use under intercropping.
questions.reference: Fundamentals of Agriculture, Vol. 1 - Arun Katyayan, Book page 137.

questions.question: Which of the following crop is the most suitable to be used as a contingency crop in dryland areas with late monsoon
questions.subject: Dryland Agriculture / Contingency Cropping
questions.explanation: Pearl millet is a short-duration, drought-tolerant cereal suited to low and uncertain rainfall. It can be sown after delayed onset of monsoon and is therefore suitable as a contingency crop in dryland areas.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 39.

questions.question: Pheromone traps are primarily used for
questions.subject: Entomology / Integrated Pest Management
questions.explanation: Pheromone traps attract insects by using species-specific chemical signals. They are mainly used to detect pest occurrence, monitor adult population and determine the appropriate time for control measures.

questions.question: Which farming practice complements the use of bird perches to maximize pest control

options.B: Habitat diversification and planting flowering plants

questions.subject: Entomology / Ecological Pest Management
questions.explanation: Bird perches encourage insectivorous birds in crop fields. Their effect is strengthened by habitat diversification and flowering plants, which provide shelter, nectar and pollen for predators and parasitoids.

questions.question: Female citrus nematode (Tylenchulus semipenetrans) produce gelatinous matrix through
questions.subject: Nematology / Citrus Nematode
questions.explanation: The mature female citrus nematode produces a gelatinous matrix around the posterior part of its body. The matrix is secreted through the excretory pore and contains the eggs.

questions.question: Which of the following groups of entomopathogenic nematodes are highly pathogenic to insects
questions.subject: Nematology / Biological Control
questions.explanation: Species of Steinernema and Heterorhabditis are highly pathogenic to insects. They enter the insect host, release symbiotic bacteria and cause rapid death of the insect.

questions.question: Scientific name of Lesion/meadow nematode is
questions.subject: Nematology / Plant-Parasitic Nematodes
questions.explanation: Pratylenchus species are commonly called lesion or meadow nematodes. They are migratory endoparasites that enter roots, feed within cortical tissues and produce elongated necrotic lesions.

questions.question: Which type of tomato plant requires staking or trellising for support?
questions.subject: Vegetable Science / Tomato
questions.topic: Growth habit and staking in tomato
questions.explanation: Indeterminate tomato plants continue growth of the main stem and produce successive flower clusters. Training, pruning and staking are followed in indeterminate types to support the continuously growing vines.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 104.

questions.question: Bitterness in brinjal is due to
questions.subject: Vegetable Science / Brinjal
questions.explanation: Bitterness in brinjal is associated with steroidal glycoalkaloids, particularly solasodine and related compounds. Higher concentration of these alkaloids gives the fruit an undesirable bitter taste.

questions.question: Black rot in cauliflower is caused by
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Vegetable Diseases
questions.explanation: Black rot of cauliflower and other crucifers is a bacterial disease. Infection commonly begins from leaf margins and produces yellow, V-shaped lesions followed by blackening of the veins.

questions.question: Which of the following spore types of the rust fungus Puccinia graminis do not cause infection directly?
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Rust Fungi
questions.explanation: Teliospores of Puccinia graminis are thick-walled resting spores. They germinate to produce a basidium and basidiospores, but the teliospores themselves do not directly infect the host plant.

questions.question: Increased concentration of which of the following gases is used for controlled atmosphere storage of crop produce?
questions.subject: Post-harvest Technology
questions.explanation: Controlled-atmosphere storage uses reduced oxygen and an increased concentration of carbon dioxide around the produce. This slows respiration, delays ripening and suppresses many storage organisms.

questions.question: Obligate fungi can be maintained for long time by

options.C: Regularly re-inoculating them on their hosts

questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Mycology
questions.explanation: Obligate fungi grow and reproduce only in living host tissue and cannot be maintained indefinitely on ordinary artificial media. They are preserved by repeatedly transferring or re-inoculating them on susceptible living hosts.

questions.question: What is the shape of Acervuli?
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Fungal Fruiting Bodies
questions.explanation: An acervulus is a shallow, saucer-shaped asexual fruiting body formed beneath the host epidermis. At maturity it ruptures the surface and exposes a mass of conidia.

questions.question: Fungicide specific to rusts
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Fungicides
questions.explanation: Plantvax is the trade name of oxycarboxin, a systemic fungicide particularly effective against rust diseases. It is absorbed by the plant and inhibits development of the rust fungus.

questions.question: Plant disease is usually measured in cases where there is complete loss of yield in a plant due to the disease.
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Disease Assessment
questions.explanation: Disease incidence expresses the proportion or percentage of plant units that are diseased. It is appropriate when each plant or unit is classified simply as diseased or healthy, including diseases that cause complete loss of an affected plant.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is generally NOT polycyclic?
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Epidemiology
questions.explanation: Smut diseases are generally monocyclic because the pathogen completes one effective infection cycle during a crop season. Rusts, leaf spots and powdery mildews commonly produce repeated secondary cycles.

questions.question: Ultra solar heat treatment is used for management of which of the following disease of wheat?
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Disease Management
questions.topic: Solar heat treatment of wheat seed
questions.explanation: Solar or ultra-solar heat treatment is used to manage internally seed-borne loose smut of wheat. Moist seed is exposed to solar heat so that the dormant mycelium is killed without destroying seed viability.

questions.question: Black heart of potato is caused due to tissue lack of
questions.subject: Post-harvest Physiology / Potato
questions.explanation: Black heart is an internal physiological disorder of potato caused by deficient oxygen supply in the tuber tissues. Poor ventilation and high respiration create an oxygen shortage, and the central tissue becomes dark or black.

questions.question: Viruses can be inactivated by
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Virology
questions.explanation: Plant viruses can be inactivated by heat when exposed above their thermal inactivation point for a sufficient period. Insecticides and antibiotics do not directly inactivate virus particles.

questions.question: Stem gall of coriander is caused by
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Spice Crop Diseases
questions.explanation: Stem gall of coriander is caused by Protomyces macrosporus. The pathogen produces swellings and galls on stems, petioles and other aerial parts of the plant.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 193.

questions.question: Pedocals are soil of non-leaching environment of __________?
questions.subject: Soil Science / Pedology
questions.explanation: Pedocals develop under arid and semi-arid climates where rainfall is insufficient for complete leaching. Calcium carbonate and other bases accumulate in the soil profile.

questions.question: __________ is an iron-rich, humus-poor mixture of clay with quartz and other diluents?
questions.subject: Soil Science / Soil Taxonomy
questions.explanation: Plinthite is an iron-rich, humus-poor mixture of clay with quartz and other diluents. On repeated wetting and drying it may harden irreversibly into ironstone.

questions.question: Plant available water held by soil between
questions.subject: Soil Science / Soil Water
questions.explanation: Field capacity is the upper limit of available water, while permanent wilting point is its lower limit. Therefore, plant-available water is the water retained in soil between field capacity and permanent wilting point.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book pages 122-123.

questions.question: Internal cork of apple is due to deficiency of which of the following nutrient?
questions.subject: Horticulture / Fruit Crops
questions.explanation: Internal cork is a physiological disorder of apple associated with boron deficiency. Corky, brown internal tissues develop because boron is essential for normal cell-wall formation and fruit tissue development.

questions.question: Blue green algae is an example of __________ N₂ fixation?
questions.subject: Soil Microbiology / Biofertilizers
questions.explanation: Blue-green algae are free-living algal inoculants that fix atmospheric nitrogen without forming a direct symbiotic association with a crop plant. They are therefore classified as non-symbiotic nitrogen fixers.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 214.

questions.question: Rock Phosphate is a
questions.subject: Soil Science / Fertilizers
questions.explanation: Rock phosphate is an insoluble phosphatic fertilizer in water but its phosphorus is evaluated through citrate solubility. It is therefore grouped as a citrate-soluble phosphatic fertilizer and is most effective in acid soils.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 202.

questions.question: Which of the following is also called as “Kisan Khad”?
questions.subject: Soil Science / Fertilizers
questions.explanation: Calcium ammonium nitrate is also known as nitro-chalk, lime nitrate or Kisan Khad. It contains ammonium nitrate mixed with calcium carbonate, which reduces its acid-forming effect.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 201.

questions.question: What is the critical limit of zinc in soil?
questions.subject: Soil Science / Micronutrients
questions.explanation: A soil-test zinc concentration of about 0.6 ppm is commonly used as the critical limit. Soils testing below this level are likely to respond to zinc application.

questions.question: The nitrification progresses well when the water content is __________ of the water-holding capacity.
questions.subject: Soil Microbiology / Nitrogen Transformation
questions.explanation: Nitrification proceeds best in a well-aerated, moist soil. A water content of about one-third to one-half of the soil water-holding capacity provides sufficient moisture while retaining air-filled pores for nitrifying organisms.

questions.question: Spongy tissue is very common disorder in which of following cultivar of mango?
questions.subject: Horticulture / Mango
questions.explanation: Spongy tissue is especially common in Alphonso mango. The affected portion of the mesocarp remains pale, sour and spongy, even though the fruit appears externally normal and ripe.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 101.

questions.question: Bunchy top is a major problem in which fruit crop?
questions.subject: Horticulture / Fruit Crop Diseases
questions.explanation: Bunchy top is a serious viral disease of banana. Infected plants produce narrow, erect and crowded leaves at the top, giving the characteristic bunchy-top appearance, and severely affected plants fail to produce a normal bunch.
questions.reference: TNAU Agritech Portal - Banana Bunchy Top disease.

questions.question: Apple scab is caused by which of following fungus?
questions.subject: Plant Pathology / Fruit Diseases
questions.explanation: Apple scab is caused by Venturia inaequalis. The fungus produces olive-green to dark scabby lesions on leaves and fruits and may cause cracking and premature leaf fall.

questions.question: Which of the following is quickest method to raise lawn in home garden?
questions.subject: Floriculture and Landscaping
questions.explanation: Turfing establishes a lawn by laying ready-made pieces of grass turf over prepared soil. Because an already developed grass cover is placed directly on the site, it is the quickest method of raising a lawn.

questions.question: Which of the following is a climbing annual with fragrant flowers
questions.subject: Floriculture / Annual Flowers
questions.explanation: Sweet pea is a climbing winter annual valued for its showy and fragrant flowers. Its weak stems climb with the help of tendrils and require support.

questions.question: Which of the following tree is grown for ornamental yellow flowers in Haryana?
questions.subject: Floriculture / Ornamental Trees
questions.explanation: Cassia fistula is an ornamental tree bearing long, pendulous clusters of bright yellow flowers. It is well adapted to warm conditions and is commonly planted along roads and in gardens.

questions.question: Trogoderma granarium belongs to which family?
questions.subject: Entomology / Stored-grain Pests
questions.explanation: Trogoderma granarium, the khapra beetle, belongs to the family Dermestidae. Its hairy larvae are the damaging stage and feed on stored grains and grain products.

questions.question: Which layer of the insect integument lacks chitin?
questions.subject: Entomology / Insect Morphology
questions.explanation: The epicuticle is the thin outermost layer of the insect cuticle and lacks chitin. Chitin occurs in the underlying procuticle, which is differentiated into exocuticle and endocuticle.

questions.question: What type of antennae are found in male and female mosquitoes
questions.subject: Entomology / Insect Antennae
questions.explanation: Male mosquitoes possess densely feathered, plumose antennae that help detect female wing-beat vibrations. Female mosquitoes have less hairy, pilose antennae.

questions.question: The monophagous insect-pest of mango, which is also of quarantine importance is
questions.subject: Entomology / Mango Pests
questions.explanation: Mango nut weevil is a monophagous pest restricted to mango. The grub develops inside the stone, and the pest is of quarantine importance because infestation may remain concealed within apparently healthy fruits.

questions.question: Paurometabolous metamorphosis is characteristic of insects belonging to orders:
questions.subject: Entomology / Insect Metamorphosis
questions.explanation: In paurometabolous insects, the immature stages are terrestrial nymphs that resemble the adults and develop external wing pads. Orthopteran insects such as grasshoppers show this type of gradual metamorphosis.

questions.question: Which body parts are regulated by the suboesophageal ganglion in insects?
questions.subject: Entomology / Insect Nervous System
questions.explanation: The suboesophageal ganglion lies below the oesophagus and is formed from the mandibular, maxillary and labial ganglia. It controls the mouthparts, salivary glands and associated feeding movements.

questions.question: Grinding of food in the insect digestive system is carried out by
questions.subject: Entomology / Insect Digestive System
questions.explanation: The proventriculus or gizzard is the posterior part of the foregut. Its cuticular teeth and plates grind food and regulate its passage into the midgut.

questions.question: Fruit flies pupate in the
questions.subject: Entomology / Fruit Flies
questions.explanation: Fruit-fly maggots leave the infested fruit after completing larval development, drop to the ground and pupate in the soil. The adult later emerges from the puparium.

questions.question: What is the full form of SC formulation?
questions.subject: Pesticide Science / Formulations
questions.explanation: SC denotes suspension concentrate. It is a stable suspension of finely divided solid active ingredient in a liquid medium and is diluted with water before spraying.

questions.question: Scientific name of Sugarcane top borer is
questions.subject: Entomology / Sugarcane Pests
questions.explanation: The sugarcane top borer is Scirpophaga excerptalis. Its larvae enter through the leaf midrib and bore towards the growing point, producing shot holes, dead heart or bunchy-top symptoms.
questions.reference: TNAU Agritech Portal - Pest of Sugarcane: Top shoot borer.

questions.question: The parapheromone of pink boll worm is
questions.subject: Entomology / Pest Monitoring
questions.explanation: Hexalure is used as a synthetic attractant in traps for monitoring male pink bollworm moths. Trap catches help determine pest activity and the timing of management measures.

questions.question: Which organization succeeded the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT)?

options.C: United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / International Trade
questions.explanation: The World Trade Organization succeeded the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade. The WTO provides the institutional framework for administering multilateral trade agreements and resolving trade disputes.

questions.question: Agricultural finance refers to:

options.B: Finance required by banks for rural lending

questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Finance
questions.explanation: Agricultural finance concerns the acquisition and use of funds required for agricultural production and allied activities. It includes credit needed for inputs, machinery, land improvement and other farm operations.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a perfectly competitive market?

options.B: A single seller controls the market.
options.D: There are significant barriers to entry.

questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Market Structure
questions.explanation: A perfectly competitive market contains many buyers and sellers dealing in a homogeneous product, with free entry and exit. No individual firm can influence the market price, so each firm acts as a price taker.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a factor affecting marketable surplus?
questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Marketing
questions.explanation: Marketable surplus is the quantity remaining after meeting family consumption, seed, feed, wages and other farm requirements. It is directly influenced by output, holding size and the producer household’s consumption requirements.

questions.question: What does market integration refer to?

options.A: Expansion of firms by consolidating additional marketing functions under a single management
options.B: Merging products under a single market structure

questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Marketing
questions.explanation: Integration in marketing occurs when related marketing functions or stages are brought under unified control. Expansion by adding successive marketing functions under one management is vertical integration.

questions.question: Agricultural production economics primarily deals with

options.B: The application of economic principles to optimize agricultural production

questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Production Economics
questions.explanation: Agricultural production economics applies economic principles to decisions about the combination and allocation of scarce farm resources. Its objective is to obtain the most profitable or efficient level of agricultural production.

questions.question: Integrated farming systems aim to:

options.A: Maximize output from a single crop
options.B: Integrate various agricultural activities to optimize resource use and income

questions.subject: Agronomy / Farming Systems
questions.explanation: An integrated farming system combines compatible enterprises such as crops, livestock, fishery, horticulture or poultry so that farm resources and by-products are efficiently recycled. This integration improves resource-use efficiency, reduces risk and supports higher and more regular farm income.
questions.reference: ICAR - Integrated Farming System resources and success stories.

questions.question: The 7Ps of marketing include all EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Marketing Management
questions.explanation: The seven Ps of marketing are product, price, place, promotion, people, process and physical evidence. Profit is an objective or outcome of marketing activity, but it is not one of the seven elements of the marketing mix.

questions.question: Which city in Haryana is famous for having one of the largest timber markets in Asia?
questions.subject: Forestry / Forest Products Marketing
questions.explanation: Yamuna Nagar is a major wood-processing and timber-trading centre of Haryana and is widely known for its large timber market and plywood industry.

questions.question: The headquarters of Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE) is at
questions.subject: Forestry / Research Institutions
questions.explanation: The Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education is the apex body of the national forestry research system, and its headquarters is located at Dehra Dun, Uttarakhand.
questions.reference: ICFRE official website - History and Headquarters.

questions.question: What is the formula to calculate the girth of a tree?
questions.subject: Forestry / Forest Mensuration
questions.topic: Girth or circumference of a tree
questions.explanation: Tree girth is the circumference of the stem. For a circular stem, circumference is calculated as 2πr; because diameter is twice the radius, the same value may also be written as πD.

questions.question: Gregor John Mendel is known as father of genetics and he was successful in his experiments because of

options.C: He chose one trait at a time in his studies

questions.subject: Genetics / Mendelian Genetics
questions.explanation: Mendel studied clear contrasting characters in garden pea and followed the inheritance of individual characters systematically. Studying distinct traits separately made segregation patterns easier to identify and analyse.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book pages 147-149.

questions.question: Double monosomy
questions.subject: Genetics / Cytogenetics
questions.explanation: A monosomic individual lacks one chromosome from a homologous pair and is represented as 2n-1. Double monosomy means that one chromosome is missing from each of two different chromosome pairs, so it is represented as 2n-1-1.

questions.question: Most widely used breeding method in crop plants is
questions.subject: Genetics and Plant Breeding
questions.explanation: In the pedigree method, desirable plants are selected generation after generation from segregating populations, and the ancestry of each selected line is recorded. It is one of the most commonly used methods for improving self-pollinated crops.

questions.question: If the recombination frequency is 5% between two loci, what will be the distance between them in terms of centimorgan unit
questions.subject: Genetics / Linkage Mapping
questions.explanation: One per cent recombination between two loci is equal to one map unit or one centimorgan. Therefore, a recombination frequency of 5 per cent corresponds to a map distance of 5 cM.

questions.question: Fundamental molecular mechanism that explains genetic recombination occurs between homologous DNA molecules proposed by
questions.subject: Genetics / Molecular Recombination
questions.explanation: Robin Holliday proposed a molecular model of homologous recombination in which homologous DNA molecules exchange strands and form a cross-shaped intermediate known as the Holliday junction.

questions.question: Plant ideotype concept was proposed by
questions.subject: Genetics and Plant Breeding
questions.explanation: The crop ideotype concept was proposed by C. M. Donald. An ideotype is an idealized plant type with a specified combination of morphological and physiological characters expected to give high performance in a particular environment.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 8.

questions.question: Simultaneous improvement of two populations is possible in
questions.subject: Genetics and Plant Breeding
questions.explanation: Reciprocal recurrent selection improves two populations simultaneously by selecting individuals from each population on the basis of their combining ability with the other population. Improved selected plants are recombined within their respective populations.

questions.question: Presence of B-line (maintainer-line) genotype in the A-line (female line) is called as
questions.subject: Seed Production / Hybrid Seed Production
questions.explanation: In a male-sterile A-line seed-production plot, a plant that carries maintainer-line fertility and produces pollen is called a pollen shedder. Such plants must be removed because they contaminate hybrid seed production.

questions.question: Which design suited for testing large germplasm?
questions.subject: Agricultural Statistics / Experimental Design
questions.explanation: An augmented design is suitable when a large number of new germplasm entries must be tested but seed is limited. Test entries are generally unreplicated, while standard checks are replicated to estimate experimental variation.

questions.question: Goodness of fit can be tested with the help of
questions.subject: Agricultural Statistics
questions.explanation: The chi-square test compares observed frequencies with frequencies expected under a stated hypothesis. It is therefore used to test goodness of fit, including the agreement of observed genetic ratios with expected Mendelian ratios.

questions.question: PPVFRA Head Quarters is located at
questions.subject: Seed Legislation / Plant Variety Protection
questions.explanation: The Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers’ Rights Authority is located at the NASC Complex, New Delhi. It administers plant-variety registration and the protection of breeders’ and farmers’ rights under the PPV&FR Act.
questions.reference: PPV&FR Authority official website - About Us and Contact Us.

questions.question: Which one is not a dominant marker?
questions.subject: Genetics / Molecular Markers
questions.explanation: RFLP and SSR markers are co-dominant because both alleles at a locus can be distinguished in a heterozygote. AFLP and ISSR are generally scored as dominant presence-or-absence markers. Therefore, both RFLP and SSR are not dominant markers.

questions.question: How farmers modify crop microclimate to improve productivity?

options.A: By planting only one type of crop in their farm
options.B: By changing soil composition in their farm
options.C: By altering atmospheric pressure surrounding their farm
options.D: By using windbreaks and mulching in their farm

questions.subject: Agricultural Meteorology
questions.explanation: Windbreaks reduce wind speed and modify temperature, humidity and evaporation around crops. Mulches cover the soil surface, reduce evaporation, moderate soil temperature and alter the near-surface microclimate.

questions.question: What is the main cause of climate change globally?

options.B: Natural variations in Earth’s orbit by natural disaster
options.C: Changes in ocean currents (Arabian & Bay of Bengal ocean)

questions.subject: Environmental Science / Climate Change
questions.topic: Primary cause of global climate change
questions.explanation: The enhanced greenhouse effect results from increasing concentrations of greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide, methane and nitrous oxide. These gases trap outgoing long-wave radiation and raise the average temperature of the atmosphere.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book pages 30-31.

questions.question: GIS is commonly used in agriculture for
questions.subject: Remote Sensing and GIS
questions.explanation: A geographic information system stores, integrates and analyses spatially referenced information. In agriculture it is used to prepare and analyse maps of land use, soils, crops, irrigation and other geographically distributed resources.

questions.question: The term extension education was first used in which year?
questions.subject: Agricultural Extension
questions.topic: Origin of the term extension education
questions.explanation: The term extension education came into use in 1873 in connection with university extension work. It later developed into an organized educational process for taking useful knowledge beyond formal institutions.

questions.question: Lab to land programme was launched by
questions.subject: Agricultural Extension / ICAR Programmes
questions.explanation: The Lab to Land Programme was launched by ICAR in 1979, its golden-jubilee year, to transfer proven agricultural technologies and skills from research institutions to farm families.
questions.reference: Guide for Agricultural Competitive Examinations - R. K. Sharma et al., Book page 13.

questions.question: Speaker, Speech and audience are related to
questions.subject: Agricultural Extension / Communication
questions.explanation: Aristotle’s model is a speaker-centred communication model consisting mainly of the speaker, the speech and the audience. It emphasizes effective public speaking and the influence of the speaker on listeners.

questions.question: How many stages identified in adoption process by the North Central Rural Sociology Subcommittee for the study of the diffusion of farm practices?
questions.subject: Agricultural Extension / Diffusion and Adoption
questions.explanation: The adoption process consists of five stages: awareness, interest, evaluation, trial and adoption. These stages describe the sequence through which an individual moves from first learning about an innovation to accepting and using it.

questions.question: Which one is an element of extension programme planning?
questions.subject: Agricultural Extension / Programme Planning
questions.explanation: Extension programme planning includes collection of facts, analysis of the situation, identification of problems and objectives, preparation and execution of a plan of work, and evaluation of progress. Therefore, all the listed activities are elements of programme planning.

questions.question: Entrepreneur is derived from ______ word.
questions.subject: Entrepreneurship Development
questions.explanation: The word entrepreneur is derived from the French verb entreprendre, meaning to undertake. An entrepreneur undertakes an enterprise, organizes resources and bears the risks associated with the activity.

questions.question: Who is minister of Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Government of India?
questions.subject: Agricultural Economics / Current Affairs
questions.topic: Union Minister for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
questions.explanation: Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi was the Union Minister of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises at the time of the 2025 examination.
questions.reference: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Government of India - Minister Profile and Who’s Who.

questions.question: Minimum genetic purity percent in foundation seed is
questions.subject: Seed Science and Technology
questions.topic: Genetic purity standard of foundation seed
questions.explanation: The minimum genetic purity prescribed for foundation seed is 99%, so option A is correct.
questions.reference: TNAU Agritech Portal - Seed Multiplication and Seed Certification.

questions.question: In India certification of seed bags for sale is
questions.subject: Seed Legislation and Certification
questions.topic: Nature of seed certification in India
questions.explanation: Seed certification is voluntary in India. A seed producer may obtain certification by meeting prescribed field and seed standards, whereas truthful labelling is compulsory for notified kinds and varieties sold as seed.
questions.reference: TNAU Agritech Portal - Seed Multiplication and Seed Certification.

questions.question: Seed coat peroxidase test is used in
questions.subject: Seed Science and Technology
questions.explanation: The seed-coat peroxidase test is used in soybean to distinguish varieties on the basis of peroxidase enzyme activity in the seed coat. The reaction produces a characteristic colour response and is useful in varietal identification and genetic-purity testing.
